description: Typecheck or build passed, but the browser route still failed due to runtime, asset, or network behavior.
body: Failure mode: implementation is accepted after static checks even though the actual route throws console errors, fails required requests, renders a blank canvas, misses an asset, or never leaves loading state. Diagnostic trigger: confidence relies on build output while user-facing DOM/CSS/browser APIs changed. Do-not-use signal: static validation alone is insufficient for visible UI changes. Recovery: open the affected route, inspect page errors and failed network requests, and tie each browser failure to source or environment cause. Risk boundary: avoid silencing console errors or swallowing promise rejections only to make smoke output cleaner; fix the visible behavior or report the external blocker.
